concepts.name: Language & Alphabet: reading, writing, phonetics basics
concepts.description: Introduction to language fundamentals: letters/alphabet (or characters), phonetics, basic reading and writing skills, understanding of simple words and sentences; basic grammar (nouns, verbs, simple sentence structure).

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide interactive drills for letter recognition, phoneme-sound mapping, basic word formation; simple reading comprehension exercises; writing practice.

learning_objectives: Recognize and write letters/characters (alphabet).
Understand basic phonetics — map letters to sounds.
Read simple words and sentences; write basic sentences.

concepts.name: Basic Grammar & Sentence Structure
concepts.description: Teach parts of speech (noun, verb, adjective, adverb, pronoun, simple prepositions/conjunctions), basic sentence syntax (subject-verb-object), simple punctuation (period, comma, question mark); building correct sentences for communication.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide exercises for sentence building, grammar drills (identify parts of speech), playful sentence rearrangements to correct grammar, punctuation practice.

learning_objectives: Identify basic parts of speech in simple sentences.
Construct grammatically correct simple sentences.
Use correct punctuation in simple writing.

concepts.name: Reading Comprehension & Basic Writing (short stories, paragraphs)
concepts.description: Develop early reading comprehension skills — read short texts (stories, paragraphs), understand meaning, infer simple ideas; basic writing: write short paragraphs, express simple ideas in writing clearly.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Present short reading passages; provide comprehension questions; allow user to write simple paragraphs or summaries; practice expression and understanding.

learning_objectives: Read and understand short texts; answer questions about content.
Summarize or paraphrase ideas from reading.
Write simple paragraphs expressing ideas clearly.

concepts.name: Introduction to Visual Arts: shapes, colors, drawing basics
concepts.description: Early exposure to visual arts — basic shapes, colors, drawing simple forms, understanding visual composition, color theory fundamentals (primary/secondary colors), recognize lines/shapes/space in art or illustrations.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide a drawing canvas for users to draw shapes, practice color mixing, basic composition tasks; simple exercises to understand visual elements.

learning_objectives: Recognize basic shapes, colors, and visual elements.
Draw simple forms and practice color mixing.
Understand basic visual composition (space, shape, color).

concepts.name: Introduction to Music: rhythm, melody, basic instruments or voice
concepts.description: Basic musical literacy: understanding rhythm, beat, simple melodies, singing or simple instrument playing; recognize basic musical patterns; simple notation (if applicable), and appreciation of sounds and music structure.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide simple rhythm and melody exercises; allow user to clap or produce beats, recognize intervals; simple singing or instrument simulation to practice melody and rhythm.

learning_objectives: Understand basic rhythm, beat, and melody.
Recognize simple musical patterns.
Perform simple melodies or rhythms using voice or virtual instrument.

concepts.name: Grammar & Writing: paragraphs, essays, grammar, vocabulary expansion
concepts.description: Advance writing skills: structured paragraphs, basic essays, coherent writing, correct grammar & syntax, vocabulary growth, basic style; reading moderately complex texts (stories, essays, articles) and understanding context, tone, main idea.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide writing prompts for paragraphs and short essays; allow vocabulary drills; reading comprehension tasks with moderate complexity; grammar correction tools/exercises.

learning_objectives: Write coherent paragraphs and short essays with correct grammar and structure.
Expand vocabulary and use varied word choice.
Read and comprehend moderately complex texts; analyze main ideas and tone.

concepts.name: Literature Appreciation: stories, poetry, drama, basic literary analysis
concepts.description: Introduce literature: reading short stories, poems, simple dramas; understanding narrative structure, themes, characters, tone, imagery, metaphor; basic literary analysis — summarizing plot, identifying themes, interpreting meaning.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide text passages (stories, poems, drama excerpts); allow user to annotate, highlight metaphors/themes; ask guided questions for interpretation; provide writing prompts for reflection or analysis.

learning_objectives: Read and understand stories, poems, and drama excerpts.
Identify plot, character, themes, literary devices (metaphor, imagery, tone).
Interpret and analyze meaning; write short reflections or analyses.

concepts.name: Visual Arts: drawing, painting, composition, color theory intermediate
concepts.description: Develop visual art skills: drawing realistically, basic shading, color mixing, composition principles (balance, contrast, focal point), perspective drawing (basic), understanding of different media (pencil, watercolor, digital).

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide drawing/painting digital canvas with tools; exercises on shading, perspective, composition; color mixing tasks; create simple artworks using learned techniques.

learning_objectives: Draw and paint with basic realistic shading and perspective.
Apply composition principles to visual artwork.
Use color theory to mix colors and create visually balanced images.

concepts.name: Music Theory & Practice: reading music, rhythms, melody & harmony basics
concepts.description: Learn musical notation, rhythms, scales, basic harmony (chords), reading sheet music, simple instrument playing or voice practice, ear training, basic composition of simple melodies.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide exercises for reading notation, rhythm training, playing or simulating instrument/voice, composing simple melodies, ear-training tasks to recognize intervals or chords.

learning_objectives: Read basic music notation and rhythms.
Understand scales, chords, and basic harmony.
Compose or play simple melodies using learned theory.

concepts.name: Advanced Writing & Composition: essays, creative writing, critical writing, rhetoric
concepts.description: Develop strong writing skills: structured essays (analysis, argument, narrative), creative writing (stories, poetry), understanding rhetoric, audience, style, tone; research-based writing, persuasive essays, critical reflection, editing and revision skills.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide structured writing assignments (analytical essay, persuasive essay, creative writing); include tools for drafting, feedback simulations (peer review), revision cycles; practice rhetoric, structure, style, argumentation and creativity.

learning_objectives: Compose structured essays (analytical, argumentative, narrative) with coherent argument and style.
Write creative texts (stories, poetry) with voice, style, and narrative structure.
Apply rhetorical techniques, adapt tone and style for different audiences, revise and refine writing through feedback.

concepts.name: Literary Analysis & Criticism: novels, plays, poetry — thematic, structural, historical analysis
concepts.description: Advanced study of literature: reading longer works (novels, full plays, poetry collections), analyzing themes, characters, plot structure, literary devices, historical/social context, genre conventions, comparative analysis, critical essays.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide full-length texts (or excerpts) for close reading; tools for annotation, note-taking; guided analysis tasks (themes, context, characters); assignments for critical essays; simulated discussion/debate environment to explore interpretations.

learning_objectives: Perform close reading and annotate literary texts effectively.
Identify and analyze themes, literary devices, context, character development, and structure.
Write critical essays interpreting literature, compare works across authors/periods, and situate in historical/social context.

concepts.name: Visual Arts Advanced: digital art, design fundamentals, art history, composition theory
concepts.description: Advanced visual arts: digital art creation or traditional media advanced techniques, principles of design (balance, contrast, typography, layout), color theory deep dive, composition theory; also introduction to art history — movements, styles, cultural contexts, critique and analysis of artworks.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide a digital art studio for users to create artworks; assign design tasks (layout, typography, composition); provide galleries with art history content; allow users to analyze and critique artworks; optionally create art in different styles or movements.

learning_objectives: Create art with advanced techniques using digital or traditional media.
Apply design principles to produce visually balanced, effective compositions.
Understand and critique artworks in historical and cultural context; analyze style, themes, and art movements.

concepts.name: Music Composition & Theory Advanced: harmony, composition, musical analysis
concepts.description: Advanced music studies: deeper music theory (harmony, chord progressions, counterpoint, arrangement), composing original music, music analysis (structure, style, genre, history), possibly orchestration or digital music production, understanding cultural/historical context of music genres.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide composition tools (MIDI or notation), allow user to compose, arrange, and produce music; provide analysis tasks of existing works; simulate instrument sounds; study music history and styles; produce original music pieces.

learning_objectives: Compose original music pieces using harmony, melody, arrangement.
Analyze musical works (structure, genre, historical context, style).
Understand and apply advanced music theory, and produce music using digital or real instruments.

concepts.name: Literary Theory & Comparative Literature: critical theory, historical context, cross-cultural studies
concepts.description: Explore advanced literary theory (structuralism, post-structuralism, postcolonialism, feminism, critical theory), comparative literature studies across languages/cultures/eras; critical essays, theory application, textual interpretation across contexts, cross-cultural literary analysis.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide a corpus of texts across languages, cultures, and historical periods; tools for annotation, comparative textual analysis, critical writing; simulated discussion/debate environments; support research-style essays and theoretical interpretation.

learning_objectives: Apply advanced literary theories to interpret texts.
Compare and analyze literature across cultures, languages, and historical periods.
Produce scholarly critical essays using theoretical frameworks and textual evidence.

concepts.name: Digital Media, Art & Multimedia Theory: studying digital art, media, film, interactive media
concepts.description: Study modern media: digital arts, multimedia, film, interactive media — theory of media, analysis of film/literature/media, multimedia storytelling, digital art history, impact of modern media; creation and critique of digital media artworks.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide tools for creating digital media (video, animation, interactive art), allow analysis of existing media (film clips, digital artworks), support multimedia storytelling projects, and enable critique and reflection on media, culture, and society.

learning_objectives: Analyze digital art and media using media theory frameworks.
Create multimedia works (video, interactive, digital art) applying principles of storytelling, design, and media aesthetics.
Reflect on sociocultural impact of media; critique media works from social, historical, and cultural perspectives.

concepts.name: Comparative Linguistics, Language History & Cultural Studies
concepts.description: Study languages comparatively: historical linguistics, language families, etymology, language evolution; study cultural contexts, how language and culture interrelate; research writing on language change, dialects, sociolinguistics, multilingualism, translation studies.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Provide datasets of languages (vocabulary, phonetics, grammar structures), allow user to analyze language evolution, compare languages, explore cognates, reconstruct proto-forms, study sociolinguistic patterns, simulate language change over time.

learning_objectives: Compare and analyze languages for structural and historical relationships.
Research and write about language evolution, sociolinguistics, and multilingualism.
Explore translation challenges, dialect variation, and cultural implications of language differences.
